actions.description: Acquired AirTrunk, one of Asia-Pacific's largest data center platforms, in partnership with Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, for approximately $16 billion.
actions.stated_rationale: Positions Blackstone as a leader in AI-driven data center infrastructure in Asia-Pacific, capitalizing on surging demand for compute capacity. Complements existing QTS data center platform.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added significant APAC data center capacity to portfolio. QTS plus AirTrunk portfolio exceeds $70B in combined data center assets.

actions.description: Launched Blackstone Private Equity Strategies Fund (BXPE), an evergreen semi-liquid fund providing individual investors access to Blackstone's PE portfolio through a lower minimum investment vehicle.
actions.stated_rationale: Extends the private wealth product suite beyond real estate (BREIT) and credit (BCRED) into private equity. Addresses growing demand from wealth channel for PE access through semi-liquid structures.
actions.observable_metric_impact: BXPE surpassed $2B AUM within its first year (by January 2025). Contributed to 53% YoY growth in private wealth fundraising.

actions.description: BCRED (Blackstone Private Credit Fund) achieved record full-year gross sales exceeding $14 billion in 2025, establishing itself as the dominant private credit vehicle in the wealth channel.
actions.stated_rationale: Capitalize on rising demand for private credit among individual investors. Perpetual capital structure provides stable fee stream and enables patient capital deployment.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record gross sales >$14B in FY2025. Q4 2025 gross sales of $3.3B. BCRED is among the largest private credit vehicles globally.

actions.description: Announced plans to launch a publicly traded acquisition vehicle focused on AI data centers, providing retail investors direct access to AI infrastructure.
actions.stated_rationale: Democratize access to AI infrastructure investments for millions of individual investors. Leverage Blackstone's data center expertise (QTS, AirTrunk) in a listed format.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Targeted for 2026 launch. Expected to deploy billions into data center acquisitions. Represents novel listed vehicle structure for alternative asset manager.

actions.description: Accelerated private wealth distribution expansion, increasing dedicated wealth professionals and deepening penetration into RIA sub-channel alongside wirehouse partners. Private wealth fundraising grew 53% YoY to $43B.
actions.stated_rationale: Capture estimated 50% market share of private wealth revenue across alternative firms. RIA channel represents under-penetrated growth opportunity.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Private wealth fundraising: $43B in 2025 (+53% YoY). Estimated ~50% market share of all private wealth alternative revenue.

actions.description: Completed full consolidation of Global Atlantic Financial Group, acquiring the remaining minority stake to achieve 100% ownership of the insurance platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Full ownership provides complete alignment and enables deeper integration of Global Atlantic's insurance capital with KKR's investment platform. Simplifies corporate structure and captures full economics.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Total insurance AUM reached $201B. Insurance economics of $1.9B (net of compensation) in 2025, up 15% YoY. Perpetual capital from insurance now 43% of total AUM.

actions.description: Agreed to acquire Arctos Partners, the largest institutional investor in professional sports franchises and a leader in GP solutions, for $1.4 billion initial consideration plus up to $550M in performance-based earnouts.
actions.stated_rationale: Establishes new KKR Solutions platform for sports, GP solutions, and secondaries. Arctos' origination network complements KKR's PE, credit, infrastructure, and capital markets capabilities.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Creates new KKR Solutions unit led by Arctos co-founder Ian Charles. Adds sports franchise and GP solutions capabilities. Target: $100B+ AUM for Solutions vertical.

actions.description: Converted an existing vehicle to KKR's asset-based finance fund (K-ABF), launching an evergreen product providing individual investors access to the ABF asset class.
actions.stated_rationale: ABF market is larger than direct lending, syndicated lending, and high-yield bond markets combined. Democratizes access to a high-growth private credit segment that was previously institutional-only.
actions.observable_metric_impact: ABF AUM grew 20% YoY to $75B. K-ABF launch in December 2025 expands retail access. Market projected to grow from $6T to $9T over four years.

actions.description: Accelerated Asia-Pacific platform builds with focused investments in Japan infrastructure, India growth equity, and Southeast Asian markets.
actions.stated_rationale: Asia-Pacific represents the fastest-growing opportunity set for infrastructure, credit, and growth equity. Japan's corporate governance reforms and India's economic growth create structural tailwinds.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $95B deployed globally in 2025, with significant allocation to Asia. Japan infrastructure and India investments contributed to record deployment pace.

actions.description: Hosted 2024 Investor Day (October 1, 2024) unveiling new five-year financial targets: SRE of $10+ per share, FRE of $12+ per share, and total adjusted net income of $15+ per share by 2029.
actions.stated_rationale: Articulate the scale of the origination-driven, credit-led growth opportunity. Demonstrate that Apollo's dual-engine model (Asset Management + Athene Retirement Services) can compound earnings at 20%+ annually.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Set 5-year targets of $15+ adjusted net income per share. On-track to reach $1T AUM by 2026. Record $228B inflows in 2025 validate trajectory.

actions.description: Scaled origination platform to record levels, exceeding $228 billion in total capital inflows for full-year 2025, driven by Athene retirement services, asset-backed finance, and direct origination.
actions.stated_rationale: Origination at scale is Apollo's core competitive advantage. By acting as a 'parallel bank' creating investment-grade assets, Apollo captures the spread that traditional banks relinquish under regulatory constraints.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record $228B inflows in FY2025. AUM reached $938.4B. On pace for $1T milestone in 2026. FRE reached record $690M quarterly.

actions.description: Expanded retirement services distribution through Athene, growing annuity and pension risk transfer volumes while increasing alternatives allocation within the insurance investment portfolio.
actions.stated_rationale: Athene provides permanent capital that grows organically through retirement product sales. Increasing alternatives allocation improves investment returns and widens the spread that generates SRE.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Athene generated $865M quarterly SRE. Deployable capital of $8.6B supports further organic and inorganic growth.

actions.description: Acquired the international business of GLP Capital Partners (GCP International) for approximately $3.7 billion in cash and $1.9 billion in Ares stock, adding $44B in real assets AUM including $18B in Japan.
actions.stated_rationale: Establishes Ares Real Estate as one of the world's largest vertically integrated platforms, nearly doubling real assets AUM to ~$96B. Provides immediate scale in Japan's real estate market through $18B of Japanese AUM, $11B in perpetual capital vehicles.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Real Assets AUM grew from ~$70B to $139.1B. Japan became a major market with $18B AUM. Nearly doubled real estate scale. 5-year AUM CAGR accelerated to 26%.

actions.description: Acquired BlueCove Limited, a London-based systematic fixed income manager, completing the full acquisition after initial minority stake taken in 2023.
actions.stated_rationale: Adds systematic/quantitative credit capabilities to complement Ares's fundamental credit platform. BlueCove AUM grew from $1.8B to $5.5B during the partnership period.
actions.observable_metric_impact: BlueCove AUM grew 3x during partnership (from $1.8B to $5.5B by Sept 2025). Adds systematic fixed income to Ares credit toolkit.

actions.description: Raised over $2.3 billion of equity commitments from third parties, Ares, and affiliates to support growth of Aspida Holdings, the insurance platform, growing Aspida to over $25.9B total assets.
actions.stated_rationale: Build captive permanent capital source through insurance. Aspida provides a growing, stable base of assets to manage across Ares's credit strategies, mirroring insurance platform models at Apollo, KKR, and Brookfield.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Aspida grew to $25.9B total assets. $2.3B equity raised. Provides permanent capital for credit deployment.

actions.description: Launched 'democratization' initiative to expand private market access for mass-affluent investors through evergreen products and expanded wealth distribution channels.
actions.stated_rationale: Mass-affluent investors represent a multi-trillion-dollar underpenetrated market for private credit. Retail-friendly structures capture long-duration capital and reduce fundraising cyclicality.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record $113B+ raised in FY2025. Management fees reached record $994M in Q4 (+27% YoY). FRE grew 33% in Q4. 20% dividend increase.

actions.description: Agreed to acquire the remaining 26% stake in Oaktree Capital Management for approximately $3 billion, achieving full ownership and integrating Oaktree's $200B+ credit platform.
actions.stated_rationale: Full integration of Oaktree creates a comprehensive credit platform alongside BAM's real assets leadership. Captures 100% of fee-related earnings and positions BAM as a top-tier credit manager.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Transaction values Oaktree at ~$11.5B. Including 100% Oaktree, BAM generated ~$2.8B FRE over LTM. BAM and BN share the acquisition costs based on fee vs. balance sheet economics.

actions.description: Launched the Brookfield Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Fund (BAIIF) targeting $10 billion in equity commitments to invest in AI infrastructure across the value chain, with up to $100 billion in total deployable capital.
actions.stated_rationale: Position Brookfield at the center of the multi-decade AI infrastructure buildout. Leverage operational expertise in power, data centers, and networks. NVIDIA and KIA are anchor co-investors, validating the thesis.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $5B initial commitments secured (including Brookfield, NVIDIA, KIA). Up to $100B total deployment capacity with co-investment and financing. Positions BAM as the leading AI infrastructure investor globally.

actions.description: Formed a $20 billion joint venture with Qai (subsidiary of Qatar Investment Authority) for AI infrastructure investments in Qatar and select international markets.
actions.stated_rationale: Partner with one of the world's largest sovereign wealth funds to deploy capital into AI infrastructure across energy, data centers, and connectivity in the Middle East and globally.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $20B joint venture scale. Deepens Middle East relationships. Complements BAIIF strategy.

actions.description: Expanded US private wealth distribution through wirehouse and RIA channels, and entered the Japanese market for private wealth products.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth represents a multi-trillion-dollar growth opportunity. US wirehouses and Japan are under-penetrated markets for real assets products.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Raised $110B+ new capital in 2025. FRE grew 22% to $3.0B. 15% dividend increase reflects fee earnings confidence.

actions.description: Acquired IPI Partners, a leading digital infrastructure investment firm managing 80+ global data centers, for approximately $1 billion.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the digital infrastructure asset class through an established platform. Data centers represent a structurally growing asset class driven by AI, cloud computing, and digitalization.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added 80+ data centers to portfolio. Established new Digital Infrastructure vertical within Blue Owl. Contributed to AUM growth from $174B to $307.4B over two years.

actions.description: Acquired Kuvare Asset Management, launching a dedicated insurance channel managing over $20 billion for insurance companies seeking investment-grade private credit.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the rapidly growing insurance-linked alternatives market. Insurance companies represent captive, long-duration capital sources ideal for Blue Owl's permanent capital model.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Established insurance channel with $20B+ AUM. Complements direct lending and net lease strategies. Adds new distribution channel.

actions.description: Acquired Atalaya Capital Management, expanding alternative credit-focused products including asset-backed lending, specialty finance, and distressed situations.
actions.stated_rationale: Broadens credit capabilities beyond direct lending into specialty credit and asset-backed strategies. Diversifies the credit platform while maintaining the permanent capital structure.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Contributed to AUM surge of 57% to $273.3B in Q1 2025. Fee-paying AUM reached $174.6B (+66% YoY). Broadened credit product suite.

actions.description: Launched a $3 billion inaugural secondaries fund focused on providing liquidity to private equity limited partners, establishing a new business vertical.
actions.stated_rationale: LP liquidity solutions represent a fast-growing market segment ($240B+ annually). Secondaries complement Blue Owl's GP stakes business, creating a comprehensive GP solutions platform.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $3B inaugural fund closed in early 2026. Establishes new LP Solutions vertical. Adds growth engine beyond direct lending and GP stakes.

actions.description: Completed the acquisition of Angelo Gordon, a $74 billion diversified credit and real estate investing platform, for approximately $2.7 billion in cash and stock.
actions.stated_rationale: Transform TPG from a PE-centric firm into a fully diversified alternatives platform. Angelo Gordon adds direct lending, asset-backed credit, CLOs, and real estate — capabilities essential for insurance and institutional capital.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added ~$74B AUM. Total AUM jumped 65% in Q2 2024 post-close. Established TPG Angelo Gordon as a major credit platform. AUM reached $303B by end-2025.

actions.description: Acquired Peppertree Capital Management, a specialist in wireless communication infrastructure (towers, data centers) with 175+ investments across ten flagship funds.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the digital infrastructure asset class through an established platform. Wireless towers and AI data centers offer mission-critical, long-duration infrastructure exposure.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added 10,000+ wireless infrastructure assets to platform. Established TPG Digital Infrastructure vertical. Diversifies real asset exposure.

actions.description: Entered into strategic insurance mandate with Jackson Financial Inc. to manage up to $20 billion in assets, with TPG investing $500 million in Jackson equity.
actions.stated_rationale: Secure sticky, long-duration insurance capital outside traditional fundraising cycles. Jackson mandate focuses on direct lending and investment-grade asset-backed finance — high-margin, recurring fee streams.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Initial mandate of $12B with potential to scale to $20B. TPG invested $500M equity in Jackson. Provides permanent capital channel for Angelo Gordon credit strategies.

actions.description: Appointed three Co-Presidents — John Redett, Mark Jenkins, and Jeff Nedelman — effective January 1, 2026, creating a new leadership structure to accelerate execution of CEO Harvey Schwartz's transformation agenda.
actions.stated_rationale: Strengthen execution capacity across the platform. Co-Presidents bring proven leadership in credit (Jenkins), PE (Nedelman), and operations/solutions (Redett) to drive firm-wide strategy alongside Schwartz.
actions.observable_metric_impact: New leadership structure supports ambitious 2028 targets: $200B inflows, $1.9B FRE, $6+ distributable EPS. Signals maturation of Schwartz transformation.

actions.description: Expanded wealth channel from 3 to 9 evergreen products and grew dedicated wealth professionals from 46 to over 110, targeting $40B wealth evergreen inflows for 2026-2028.
actions.stated_rationale: Wealth channel represents the highest-growth distribution opportunity. $40B target is 3x the $12B raised in prior three-year period, reflecting product maturation and distribution scale.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Products expanded 3x (from 3 to 9). Professionals expanded 2.4x (from 46 to 110+). $40B three-year target set. Wealth channel drove portion of record $54B FY2025 inflows.

actions.description: Partnered with KKR to acquire a $10.1 billion portfolio of prime student loans from Discover Financial Services, deploying through Carlyle's Credit Strategic Solutions team.
actions.stated_rationale: Asset-backed credit represents a growing portion of the private credit opportunity. Student loan portfolio provides predictable cash flows with investment-grade-like credit quality.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $10.1B portfolio acquired. Subsequent $1B ABS issuance validated the strategy. Demonstrates Carlyle Credit's asset-backed capabilities.

actions.description: Announced $2 billion share buyback program at the 2026 Shareholder Update, signaling confidence in intrinsic value and commitment to shareholder returns.
actions.stated_rationale: Shares trade at a discount to intrinsic value as Schwartz transformation gains traction. Buyback complements dividend growth and demonstrates capital discipline.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $2B buyback announced. Combined with 2028 targets of $6+ distributable EPS. Record FRE in FY2025 supports capital return capacity.

actions.description: Advanced Fortitude Re insurance solutions strategy, raising $2.1 billion for Fortitude Re and establishing FCA Re with over $700 million of capital for Asian insurance market expansion.
actions.stated_rationale: Insurance capital provides permanent, captive AUM. Asian insurance market (FCA Re) represents geographic expansion of insurance distribution. Advisory relationship generates recurring fees.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $2.1B raised for Fortitude Re. FCA Re established with $700M+ capital for Asia. Insurance advisory revenue growing.

actions.description: Agreed to acquire 100% of the Coller Capital management company and GP entities, plus 10% of carried interest in the most recent secondaries flagship, for approximately $3.7 billion. Coller manages nearly $50 billion.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the fast-growing secondaries market through the world's leading independent secondaries firm. Secondaries complement EQT's active ownership PE strategy and enable GP-led continuation vehicle capabilities.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Adds ~$50B AUM when closed. Establishes EQT as a leading player in the $240B+ annual secondaries market. Expected to close in 2026.

actions.description: Acquired Perficient, a global digital consultancy firm, for approximately $3 billion in an all-cash transaction.
actions.stated_rationale: Thematic investment in digitalization — a core EQT investment theme. Perficient's digital consulting capabilities complement EQT portfolio company value creation strategy.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Portfolio investment ($3B). Advances EQT's digitalization theme. All-cash structure demonstrates capital deployment capacity.

actions.description: Scaled evergreen and institutional open-ended strategies, raising EUR 1.9 billion and reaching EUR 3.5 billion NAV by end of 2025, marking a pivotal year for private wealth expansion.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth and open-ended institutional investors represent the next major growth wave. Evergreen structures address the allocation gap for investors seeking private market exposure without traditional J-curve.
actions.observable_metric_impact: EUR 1.9B raised to evergreen strategies in 2025. NAV reached EUR 3.5B. Inflection point for private wealth channel.

actions.description: Renamed BPEA (acquired in 2022) to EQT Private Capital Asia, completing the brand integration of the Asian PE platform into the EQT franchise.
actions.stated_rationale: Unified branding strengthens the EQT franchise globally. Integration of BPEA into EQT Private Capital Asia signals maturation of the 2022 acquisition and operational alignment.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Completes brand integration. Asian PE platform now fully part of EQT franchise. Supports cross-selling across EQT strategies.

actions.description: Acquired Empira Group, a leading European residential real estate investment platform with EUR 14 billion Gross Development Value, to enhance vertically integrated real estate capabilities.
actions.stated_rationale: Real estate is entering a new investment paradigm. Empira's vertically integrated residential platform in Europe fills a strategic gap and supports Partners Group's ambitious AUM growth targets.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added $4.0B one-off underwritten AUM. EUR 14B GDV portfolio. Enhances European residential real estate origination and management.

actions.description: Launched private markets royalties as a fifth asset class (May 2024), pioneering a new private markets category alongside PE, infrastructure, real estate, and private debt.
actions.stated_rationale: Royalties represent an uncorrelated return stream with inflation protection and stable cash flows. Cross-sector coverage (pharmaceuticals, energy transition, sports) creates diversification. Targets $30B+ AUM.
actions.observable_metric_impact: 5th asset class launched. Evergreen royalties fund launched for Asia, Europe, Middle East investors (May 2025) targeting 10% net returns. AUM target: $30B+.

actions.description: Made strategic investments in Trinity Investments (US hospitality-focused real estate, April 2024) and Citivale (UK logistics real estate developer, February 2024) to build vertically integrated real estate platforms.
actions.stated_rationale: Build vertically integrated origination and operating capabilities in key real estate sub-sectors. Hospitality and logistics are structurally growing segments requiring operational expertise.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Adds US hospitality and UK logistics operating platforms. Complements Empira residential acquisition. Builds toward comprehensive global real estate vertical integration.

actions.description: Expanded evergreen fund suite with seven new offerings in 2025, making it the most comprehensive private markets evergreen platform globally.
actions.stated_rationale: Address the allocation gap for private wealth and institutional investors seeking private markets access through open-ended, lower-minimum structures. Solve the 'one-stop solution' challenge for private wealth.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Seven new evergreen funds launched. Bespoke solutions and evergreens both achieved record fundraising years in 2025. 72% of capital raised came from bespoke solutions.

actions.description: Launched StepStone Private Equity Strategies Fund (STPEX), an evergreen interval fund investing in PE assets from top-tier sponsors, raising $750M+ at inception with $5,000 minimum and no accreditation requirements.
actions.stated_rationale: Address private wealth demand for PE exposure through an evergreen structure with daily valuation, low minimums, and access via all major custodian platforms. Democratize PE for non-accredited investors.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $750M+ raised at launch (September 2025). Available on all major custodian platforms. $5,000 minimum with no accreditation requirement. Semi-annual redemptions at up to 5% NAV.

actions.description: Scaled Private Wealth Solutions to $10.2 billion AUM by July 2025, doubling in under one year, becoming a significant growth engine alongside institutional mandates.
actions.stated_rationale: Private wealth represents the fastest-growing segment of private markets demand. StepStone's data and GP relationship advantages translate well to wealth channel advisory and fund selection.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Private Wealth Solutions doubled to $10.2B AUM in under one year. Contributes to 29% organic fee-earning AUM growth rate.

actions.description: Co-developed the 2026 Private Equity GP Outlook with Bain & Company, establishing thought leadership at the intersection of data analytics and private markets research.
actions.stated_rationale: Leverage StepStone's proprietary data and GP relationships to produce definitive industry research. Strengthens brand positioning and supports institutional distribution.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Published joint research report with Bain. Establishes StepStone as a data-driven thought leader. Supports GP relationship deepening.

actions.description: Scaled Evergreen Platform to $16B+ AUM across 11 evergreen funds, achieving 70%+ year-over-year growth in evergreen assets.
actions.stated_rationale: Evergreen funds are the primary vehicle for private markets democratization. Hamilton Lane's data advantage (58,000+ fund database) supports superior GP selection for evergreen portfolios.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Evergreen AUM reached $16B+ (70%+ YoY growth). 11 evergreen funds in suite. Serves thousands of advisors.

actions.description: Partnered with Republic to bring institutional-quality private market investments to individual retail investors in the US, launching the first infrastructure evergreen fund on Republic's platform with a $500 minimum investment.
actions.stated_rationale: Blockchain-based technology platform enables access at unprecedented minimum levels. Infrastructure is the first asset class to be made available to non-accredited retail investors.
actions.observable_metric_impact: HLPIF launched on Republic with $500 minimum. First private infrastructure offering for US retail investors. Uses blockchain-based digital solutions.

actions.description: Closed Hamilton Lane Infrastructure Opportunities Fund II at nearly $2 billion in total commitments (including related vehicles), tripling the size of the predecessor fund.
actions.stated_rationale: Infrastructure is a structurally growing allocation target for institutional investors. Tripling predecessor fund size demonstrates strong LP demand and track record validation.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $1.5B in the Fund plus $400M in related vehicles (~$2B total). 3x predecessor fund size. Establishes infrastructure as a core growth vertical.

actions.description: Appointed Mika Tashiro as Head of Private Wealth Solutions in Japan effective March 1, 2026, expanding distribution of evergreen private markets strategies to Japanese wealth investors.
actions.stated_rationale: Japan represents a large, underpenetrated market for private markets wealth products. Local leadership is essential for navigating Japanese financial advisor and distribution networks.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Establishes dedicated Japan private wealth leadership. Targets partnerships with Japanese wealth managers and financial institutions for evergreen strategy distribution.

actions.description: Agreed to acquire Just Group plc, a UK-based financial services company specializing in pension risk transfer, for $3.18 billion, expanding Brookfield Wealth Solutions into the UK pension market.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the UK defined benefit pension de-risking market, one of the largest globally. Just Group provides established distribution and operating capabilities in a structurally growing market.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $3.18B acquisition price. Expected to close H1 2026. Expands Wealth Solutions beyond North America into UK pension risk transfer.

actions.description: Completed the acquisition of American Equity Investment Life (AEL), a major US annuity provider, for $4.3 billion, significantly scaling Brookfield Wealth Solutions.
actions.stated_rationale: Scale the investment-led insurance model built around long-duration annuities. AEL provides access to large-scale annuity origination and investment management opportunities.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $4.3B transaction. Wealth Solutions earnings grew 24% to $1.7B in 2025. Significantly expands insurance capital base.

actions.description: Announced steps to enhance corporate structure through planned BNT merger, which will simplify the Brookfield ecosystem and broaden shareholder ownership of BAM.
actions.stated_rationale: Simplify multi-entity structure that creates complexity for investors. BNT merger broadens access to insurance earnings stream. Enhanced corporate structure intended to reduce holding company discount.
actions.observable_metric_impact: BNT merger planned. Structure simplification expected to improve investor access and potentially reduce trading discount. BAM ownership restructured to 73% by BN.

actions.description: Acquired Neoen, a global renewable energy development company, for $3.2 billion, expanding the renewable power and transition portfolio.
actions.stated_rationale: Renewable energy development is a core Brookfield capability. Neoen adds a global development pipeline and operating capacity in renewables, supporting the energy transition supercycle thesis.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $3.2B deployment. Expands renewable power development capabilities globally. Supports multi-decade decarbonization investment thesis.

actions.description: Announced long-term strategic and equity partnership with Amundi, in which Amundi acquired a 9.9% equity stake in ICG and became exclusive global wealth distributor for key ICG products.
actions.stated_rationale: Access Amundi's 200M+ individual investors globally for wealth distribution. Amundi provides distribution infrastructure that would take years and significant investment to build organically.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Amundi acquired 9.9% stake in ICG. Exclusive distribution agreement for wealth products. Target: co-developed evergreen products in H1 2026 (private credit, secondaries).

actions.description: Launched ICG Core Private Equity, an institutional-quality US evergreen fund providing clients differentiated access to PE through the secondary market.
actions.stated_rationale: US wealth market is the largest globally and requires dedicated product. Evergreen secondaries structure leverages ICG's Strategic Equity franchise (world's largest GP-led secondaries fund).
actions.observable_metric_impact: First US evergreen product. Leverages Strategic Equity V ($11B) track record. Targets US wealth channel penetration.

actions.description: Closed Senior Debt Partners V (SDP V) at $17 billion, making it the largest ever direct lending fund in Europe, and Strategic Equity V at $11 billion, the world's largest GP-led secondaries fund.
actions.stated_rationale: Validates 'breadth at scale' strategy — ability to raise market-leading funds across different strategies demonstrates institutional LP confidence and track record. Record fund sizes attract more capital.
actions.observable_metric_impact: SDP V: $17B (Europe's largest direct lending fund). Strategic Equity V: $11B (world's largest GP-led secondaries fund). Combined $28B from two flagships alone.

actions.description: Set a fundraising objective of at least $55 billion over the next four years, signaling a step-change in ambition from the $24 billion milestone FY2025.
actions.stated_rationale: Capitalize on breadth at scale positioning and Amundi partnership. Multi-year target provides visibility to investors and signals confidence in fundraising momentum.
actions.observable_metric_impact: 4-year target: $55B+ (implies ~$14B/year avg, up from $24B in FY2025 peak). 15th consecutive dividend increase to 83p/share.

actions.description: Acquired Energy Capital Partners (ECP), a leading North American infrastructure and energy transition investment firm, for approximately GBP 835 million ($1.05 billion) in cash, stock, and assumed debt.
actions.stated_rationale: Diversify from European PE into North American infrastructure and energy transition. ECP brings $20B+ infrastructure strategy, a two-decade track record, and access to the fast-growing clean energy investment cycle.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Added ~$20B AUM. Created a EUR 57B global platform. Established infrastructure as a third pillar alongside PE and credit. AUM rose 20% YoY to EUR 73.7B.

actions.description: Added Newbury Partners team and $4 billion+ in secondaries AUM, expanding into the private equity secondaries market.
actions.stated_rationale: Enter the fast-growing secondaries market through an experienced team. Secondaries provide diversification, shorter duration, and access to the GP-led continuation vehicle trend.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Adds $4B+ AUM. New Secondaries vertical established. Team expected to join early 2026. Diversifies revenue beyond PE, credit, and infrastructure.

actions.description: Deployed approximately EUR 2.0 billion in European direct lending through the Bridgepoint Direct Lending team, investing in 15 deals year-to-date in 2025.
actions.stated_rationale: European mid-market direct lending benefits from bank retrenchment. Bridgepoint's local European presence and sector expertise provide origination advantages in middle-market credit.
actions.observable_metric_impact: EUR 2.0B deployed across 15 deals. Demonstrates resilient deal volumes in European middle market. Credit platform growing as a complement to PE.

actions.description: BREIT invested $5.8 billion in pre-leased data center developments in 2025, with QTS (acquired for $10B in 2021) expanding more than eightfold and now accounting for 20.4% of BREIT asset value.
actions.stated_rationale: Data centers are the highest-conviction real estate sector. AI demand is driving unprecedented leasing activity. Blackstone's operational expertise (QTS) creates a proprietary development pipeline.
actions.observable_metric_impact: QTS expanded 8x+ since acquisition. $5.8B deployed into data center development in 2025 alone. QTS now 20.4% of BREIT. $100B prospective data center pipeline.

actions.description: Launched K-Series evergreen products (KREST for real estate, KREF for credit, K-ABF for asset-based finance) expanding retail and wealth investor access across asset classes.
actions.stated_rationale: Evergreen products capture long-duration capital from wealth investors and reduce fundraising cyclicality. K-Series brands create a unified retail-accessible product suite.
actions.observable_metric_impact: K-ABF launched December 2025. KREST and KREF operational. Supports growth of perpetual capital (rose 16% to $321B, 43% of AUM).

actions.description: Launched and scaled hybrid credit products and Fund X (private equity), generating record quarterly FRE of $690 million driven by strong inflows into both vehicles.
actions.stated_rationale: Hybrid credit provides flexible capital solutions between debt and equity, targeting returns above traditional credit with downside protection. Fund X continues the high-margin PE franchise.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record FRE of $690M quarterly. Strong Fund X and Hybrid Value inflows. Total AUM reached $938.4B.

actions.description: Integrated artificial intelligence into investment and reporting processes as part of a firm-wide initiative to drive margin expansion and improve investment decision-making.
actions.stated_rationale: AI tools can improve credit underwriting speed, portfolio monitoring, and client reporting efficiency. Technology-driven margin expansion supports FRE growth without proportional headcount increases.
actions.observable_metric_impact: FRE margin expansion contributing to 33% Q4 FRE growth. Part of broader platform supporting record $113B+ fundraising.

actions.description: Entered the Japanese market for private wealth products and expanded Middle East presence through QIA partnership, establishing new distribution channels in two of the world's largest capital pools.
actions.stated_rationale: Japan and Middle East are among the largest sources of investable capital globally. Local presence and partnerships are required to access these markets effectively.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Japan distribution established. $20B QIA AI infrastructure JV. $110B+ total capital raised in 2025 across global channels.

actions.description: Acquired Prima Capital Advisors (June 2024) to add real estate credit capabilities, broadening the credit platform beyond corporate direct lending.
actions.stated_rationale: Real estate credit provides diversification from corporate direct lending. Prima Capital adds origination and underwriting capabilities in a structurally growing market.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Contributed to 57% AUM surge in Q1 2025 (alongside Kuvare, Atalaya, IPI). Broadens credit product offering.

actions.description: Called 2025 a 'breakout year' at BofA Conference, targeting $50B+ in fundraising for 2026 as the multi-platform model achieves scale across all six strategies.
actions.stated_rationale: Post-IPO diversification strategy reaching maturity. Angelo Gordon integration, Peppertree addition, and Jackson partnership create multiple fundraising engines operating simultaneously.
actions.observable_metric_impact: AUM reached $303B (23% YoY growth). 166% AUM growth since 2021. $72B dry powder. $50B+ fundraising target for 2026.

actions.description: Harvey Schwartz completed three-year transformation: eliminated underperforming business lines, reshaped operating model, reduced expenses, and achieved record FY2025 financial results.
actions.stated_rationale: Three guiding principles: (1) strategic growth plan tied to client needs, (2) reshape operating model with expense discipline, (3) leadership and organizational design changes. Systematically transformed Carlyle into a more durable platform.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record FY2025: FRE up 12%, $54B inflows (+32%), record transaction fees $225M (+40% YoY). #1 PE sponsor globally by IPO proceeds.

actions.description: Closed the fourth Tactical Growth Fund with over $700 million in capital commitments, demonstrating continued growth in specialized co-investment and secondary strategies.
actions.stated_rationale: Tactical growth funds provide concentrated exposure to attractive co-investment and secondary opportunities identified through StepStone's GP relationship network.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $700M+ committed. Fourth fund in series. Complements managed account and evergreen product growth.

actions.description: Expanded APAC and Middle East presence through joint ventures with leading financial services firms, targeting new distribution channels for bespoke mandates and evergreen products.
actions.stated_rationale: APAC and Middle East represent the largest pools of capital seeking private markets exposure. JV model leverages local partners' distribution capabilities while Partners Group provides investment content.
actions.observable_metric_impact: $30B new assets raised in 2025. Growing proportion from APAC and Middle East. JV partnerships established for evergreen distribution.

actions.description: Partnered with YCharts to add Hamilton Lane benchmark data to the YCharts platform, providing financial advisors with proprietary private markets benchmarks drawn from fund- and deal-level LP cash-flow data.
actions.stated_rationale: Data and technology are key differentiators. Making benchmark data available on advisor platforms drives adoption of Hamilton Lane products and strengthens the firm's position as the data leader in private markets.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Benchmark data now available to advisors via YCharts. Draws on proprietary database of 58,000+ funds. Supports wealth channel growth.

actions.description: Brookfield Wealth Solutions grew earnings 24% to $1.7 billion in 2025, driven by expanded product offerings, new geographies, and investment portfolio optimization.
actions.stated_rationale: Insurance-linked permanent capital is the highest-growth segment. Geographic expansion (Just Group UK acquisition pending) and product innovation drive organic growth alongside AEL's scale.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Wealth Solutions earnings $1.7B (+24% YoY). Record distributable earnings $5.4B for BN. 15% dividend increase.

actions.description: Achieved EQT's strongest realization year ever in 2025, with EUR 16 billion in gross fund investments and EUR 14 billion in co-investments, demonstrating accelerated portfolio rotation.
actions.stated_rationale: Active exit management returns capital to LPs and supports next-generation fundraising. Record realizations in 2025 position EQT for strong fundraising cycles in 2026-2027.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Record realization year. EUR 16B gross fund investments + EUR 14B co-investments. Adjusted EBITDA margin of 60%. Higher dividends and share buybacks.

actions.description: Issued 30 million new ordinary shares as Energy Capital Partners sellers converted OP Units from the 2023 acquisition structure, with shares admitted to LSE main market in February 2026.
actions.stated_rationale: Deferred equity component of ECP acquisition structuring. Conversion aligns ECP management incentives with Bridgepoint shareholders through equity ownership.
actions.observable_metric_impact: 30M new shares issued. ECP management becomes long-term equity holders. Shares admitted to LSE February 2026.

actions.description: Acquired Excellera Advisory Group (August 2025), expanding ICG's technology and advisory capabilities.
actions.stated_rationale: Enhance technology and advisory services to support portfolio companies and improve operational efficiency across the platform.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Adds technology advisory capabilities. Supports portfolio company value creation and operational improvement.

actions.description: Expanded global origination footprint through strategic partnerships and platform investments, growing international credit origination capabilities beyond the US market.
actions.stated_rationale: Scale origination platform internationally to reach $275B+ annually. International markets offer wider spreads and less competition for investment-grade asset origination.
actions.observable_metric_impact: Total origination exceeded $228B in 2025. AUM grew 25% YoY to $938.4B. International origination contributing to growth.

actions.description: Established KKR Solutions as a new business vertical, combining Arctos acquisition capabilities with existing solutions to target $100B+ AUM for sports, GP solutions, and secondaries.
actions.stated_rationale: Create a dedicated platform for adjacent investment strategies that leverage KKR's brand and origination network. Sports, GP stakes, and secondaries represent structurally growing segments.
actions.observable_metric_impact: New KKR Solutions vertical established. Led by Arctos co-founder Ian Charles. $100B+ AUM ambition for the platform.
